Patch Set 1:

I'm fine to move this check to OSDs. Di, notice that LDLM is an additional mechanism to maintain coherency. OSD should be able to work w/o LDLM, but shouldn't assert in case of a race like this, of course. to me this case is similar to the few racing inserts into the same index - OSD internally maintain consistency and avoids duplicates.

Patch Set 1:

Sorry to give - for open discussion, since this patch makes OSP and OSD different, it is a bit important to me, please correct me if I am wrong.
Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Patch Set 1:

I agree assertion is not good, but my point is 
1. we should find the race and fix it,
2. this check makes osd/osp different, and not good to me, so better move this to OSD.

Patch Set 1:

> 1. we should find the race and fix it,

Please see the discussion on LU-5565.

Patch Set 1:

Di, please explain the difference in details.

Patch Set 1:

Please see the discussion on LU-5565.

I just post my comments there.

Patch Set 1:

I mean for local object you get assert, but for remote object it returns ENOENT.

Patch Set 1:

ok, I agree with that. in both the cases we should be getting an error.

Patch Set 1:

I see several dt_object_exists() assertions in the OSD method implementations. osd_attr_get(), osd_object_create(), osd_object_ref_add(), osd_declare_object_ref_del(), osd_object_ref_del(), osd_xattr_get(), osd_xattr_list(), osd_declare_xattr_del()...

Should we convert them all? Having these assertions is the declare methods seems especially perverse to me.

Patch Set 1:

I think we should keep dt_remote_object assertion( a bigger deal to me, since if that happens, it really means something is seriously wrong).  So probably just replacing dt_object_exists with ENOENT.

Patch Set 5:

I actually would prefer to hold this patch a bit, since current LBUG will help us find some bugs for example during racer.  Of course if this is a blocker for 2.7. I do not mind to land it now. Thanks

Patch Set 5:

There's similar assertions in osd-zfs as well, e.g. osd_attr_set() and osd_attr_get(). I wonder whether they'd need the same fix. If yes, why not move the check one layer above?
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 30b0b011dfd53faf8f903401fa0539a8d625b0e5

Patch Set 6:

> There's similar assertions in osd-zfs as well, e.g. osd_attr_set() and osd_attr_get(). I wonder whether they'd need the same fix. If yes, why not move the check one layer above?

1) There is no single "one layer above."

2) It's better to make the callee safe than to make the caller careful.

parents.subject: LU-4648 ldiskfs: delete external EA in another transaction

commit.subject: LU-5565 osd-ldiskfs: handle non-existing objects
commit.message:
LU-5565 osd-ldiskfs: handle non-existing objects

In the DT API method implementations in lustre/osd/osd_ldiskfs.c
replace assertions that the object exists (or does not exist) with
appropriate error returns.

Signed-off-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Change-Id: If346861eb352efaf0b90a8e7778e8fbb3cf16bf0
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/12608
Reviewed-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
